commands.name: create-release
commands.description:
Makes sure the tag exists as release on github and
introduces new version labels for the next
major/minor/patch

options.name: --body
options.description:
Path to file that will be read and used for the body
for the release, can be specified multiple times to
combine
<owner> <repository name>
Repository to use in <owner> <repos_name> format

options.name: --version
options.description: Version that is being released

options.name: --token
options.description:
The github token to use, if the issue list is long
this may be necessary, defaults to anonymous

options.name: --label
options.description:
Map Github labels to categorizations, format <label>
<description>, can be specified multiple times

options.name: --releasetagformat
options.description:
The release tag format, defaults to VERSION, VERSION
will be replaced by the actual version

options.name: --releaselabelformat
options.description:
The release label format, defaults to vVERSION,
VERSION will be replaced by the actual version

options.name: --backportlabelformat
options.description:
The backport label format, defaults to 'Backport
BRANCH`, BRANCH will be calculated from the version

options.name: --help
options.description: display this list of options.

commands.name: current-version
commands.description:
Given search syntax finds the current and the next
versions on separate lines

options.name: --query
options.description:
An anchor query, M.N, M.x, or master. will find the
current and next patch, minor, major respectfully
<owner> <repository name>
Repository to use in <owner> <repos_name> format

options.name: --version
options.description: Version that is being released

options.name: --token
options.description:
The github token to use, if the issue list is long
this may be necessary, defaults to anonymous

options.name: --label
options.description:
Map Github labels to categorizations, format <label>
<description>, can be specified multiple times

options.name: --releasetagformat
options.description:
The release tag format, defaults to VERSION, VERSION
will be replaced by the actual version

options.name: --releaselabelformat
options.description:
The release label format, defaults to vVERSION,
VERSION will be replaced by the actual version

options.name: --backportlabelformat
options.description:
The backport label format, defaults to 'Backport
BRANCH`, BRANCH will be calculated from the version

options.name: --help
options.description: display this list of options.

commands.name: find-previous
commands.description: Find the previous release for the passed version

options.name: --version
options.description: Version that is being released

options.name: --token
options.description:
The github token to use, if the issue list is long
this may be necessary, defaults to anonymous

options.name: --label
options.description:
Map Github labels to categorizations, format <label>
<description>, can be specified multiple times

options.name: --oldversion
options.description: optional the tool will find the previous release

options.name: --releasetagformat
options.description:
The release tag format, defaults to VERSION, VERSION
will be replaced by the actual version

options.name: --releaselabelformat
options.description:
The release label format, defaults to vVERSION,
VERSION will be replaced by the actual version

options.name: --backportlabelformat
options.description:
The backport label format, defaults to 'Backport
BRANCH`, BRANCH will be calculated from the version

options.name: --format
options.description:
The format in which to print the results, can be
markdown and asciidoc

options.name: --uncategorizedheader
options.description:
The header to use in the markdown for uncategorized
issues/prs

options.name: --output
options.description:
write the release notes to a file as well as standard
out, VERSION will be replaced by the actual version

options.name: --help
options.description: display this list of options.
